Container.Name: Dropbox
Container.Overview:
Headless Dropbox sync powered by Maestral — an open-source client built on the official Dropbox HTTP API v2. No display server required. Supports amd64 and arm64.
First-time setup: after the container starts, open the Unraid terminal and run: docker exec -it Dropbox maestral auth link Follow the on-screen link to authorise your Dropbox account. Syncing begins automatically once authorised.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: UID of the user that should own the synced files. On Unraid, 99 (nobody) is typical.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: GID of the group that should own the synced files. On Unraid, 100 (users) is typical.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Timezone — prevents file timestamp issues. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

Config.Name: DROPBOX_CONFIG_NAME
Config.Description: Maestral config profile name. Change only if running multiple Dropbox accounts.

Config.Name: DROPBOX_PATH
Config.Description: Path inside the container where Dropbox files are synced. Must match the container path of the Dropbox Files volume below.

Config.Name: Dropbox Config
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Maestral credentials and config. Must survive container restarts.

Config.Name: Dropbox Files
Config.Description: Host path where your Dropbox files will be synced.

Config.Name: LAN Sync Port
Config.Description: Optional — expose for Dropbox LAN sync with other devices on the same network.
